message:
fix(ready): exclude in_progress issues from ready work queue

The ready command is meant to surface unclaimed actionable work — issues
that are available to pick up next. Previously, `get_ready_issues()`
included `in_progress` issues in its query filter, which is semantically
wrong: in_progress means the issue has already been claimed by someone.

Changes:
- storage/sqlite.rs: Changed the ready query's status filter from
  `IN ('open', 'in_progress')` to `= 'open'` (or `IN ('open', 'deferred')`
  when include_deferred is set). This ensures only genuinely unclaimed
  work surfaces in the ready list.
- ready.rs: Improved the empty_ready_message() function to provide
  context-aware messages. When open issues exist but none are ready,
  it now says "No ready issues — all remaining work is blocked, deferred,
  or in progress" instead of the misleading "No ready issues". When no
  non-closed issues exist at all, it says "All work complete — no issues
  to work on" instead of the ambiguous "No open issues".
- storage/sqlite.rs: Added unit test `test_get_ready_issues_excludes_in_progress`
  that creates both an open and in_progress issue, verifying only the
  open one appears in ready results.
- tests/storage_ready.rs: Renamed integration test from
  `ready_includes_in_progress_and_open` to
  `ready_excludes_in_progress_and_includes_only_open` and flipped the
  assertion to verify in_progress issues are excluded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
